claims.text: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs).
claims.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

claims.text: The convolution operator possesses the properties of simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.
claims.source_quote: The convolution operator is the fundamental building block of modern convolutional neural networks (CNNs), owing to its simplicity, translational equivariance, and efficient implementation.

claims.text: The convolution operator's structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.
claims.source_quote: However, its structure as a fixed, linear, locally-averaging operator limits its ability to capture structured signal properties such as low-rank decompositions, adaptive basis representations, and non-uniform spatial dependencies.

claims.text: The paper presents a systematic taxonomy of operators that extend or replace the standard convolution in learning-based image processing pipelines.
claims.source_quote: This paper presents a systematic taxonomy of operators that extend or replace the standard convolution in learning-based image processing pipelines.

claims.text: The taxonomy organises alternative operators into five families: decomposition-based operators, adaptive weighted operators, basis-adaptive operators, integral and kernel operators, and attention-based operators.
claims.source_quote: We organise the landscape of alternative operators into five families: (i) decomposition-based operators... (ii) adaptive weighted operators... (iii) basis-adaptive operators... (iv) integral and kernel operators... and (v) attention-based operators

claims.text: Decomposition-based operators separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions.
claims.source_quote: (i) decomposition-based operators, which separate structural and noise components through singular value or tensor decompositions

claims.text: Adaptive weighted operators modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content.
claims.source_quote: (ii) adaptive weighted operators, which modulate kernel contributions as a function of spatial position or signal content

claims.text: Basis-adaptive operators optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights.
claims.source_quote: (iii) basis-adaptive operators, which optimise the analysis bases together with the network weights

claims.text: Integral and kernel operators generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels.
claims.source_quote: (iv) integral and kernel operators, which generalise the convolution to position-dependent and non-linear kernels

claims.text: Attention-based operators relax the locality assumption entirely.
claims.source_quote: (v) attention-based operators, which relax the locality assumption entirely

claims.text: The paper provides a comparative analysis of all five operator families across the dimensions of linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks.
claims.source_quote: We further provide a comparative analysis of all families across relevant dimensions -- linearity, locality, equivariance, computational cost, and suitability for image-to-image and image-to-label tasks
